finding: retry-loop termination is sound: attempted_repair set before continue + short-circuit on strip==false prevents a 2nd retry/infinite loop
sampling-omit gate is correct: Adaptive/Enabled omit temperature/top_p/top_k, Disabled/None preserve, ANDed with sampling_restricted

is_sampling_param_400 matched ANY 400 mentioning a sampling word, so an invalid-VALUE error (temperature=5.0) was treated as repairable and silently answered at the provider default, masking the misconfiguration
strip_sampling_params removes all three keys even if only one offended
note: by-design: Anthropic sampling support is all-or-nothing per model/thinking-state; narrowed predicate only repairs genuine unsupported 400s

finding: anthropic_http_error surfaced the parsed message verbatim with NO sanitize_error_body bounding/newline-collapse, bypassing the control from_status_code applied
self-heal warn! interpolated raw request.model (operator-supplied) enabling CR/LF log-forging
retry resends x-api-key to the same base_url only; key never enters error strings/logs/captured bodies
non-JSON/missing-field error bodies fall back cleanly (parse None -> from_status_code sanitized); resp.text().unwrap_or_default no panic
a non-repairable but parseable 400 was surfaced unbounded via from_anthropic_error rather than length-capped
note: same sanitize fix as the security finding

finding: the #[tokio::test] had no timeout; a non-retry regression would block server.await/accept forever, hanging CI instead of failing fast
retry loop bounded to <=2 HTTP sends; shared OnceLock reqwest client is Send+Sync and safe to reuse across iterations and concurrent calls
dropping the forced 0.7 default in the provider-generic apply_model_params also changes OpenAI/Gemini: an unset model temperature now inherits the provider default (~1.0) instead of 0.7
note: intended per task item #4 (stop force-injecting defaults, on the shared mapper); documented for human confirmation in FIX_ROUND-1 + PR

finding: retry mutates the same body Value in place and re-serializes, so content-length/payload are correct for the stripped retry; attempted_repair is fresh per call
claude-sonnet-5 supports_sampling_params:false is a product assumption; if wrong it silently strips operator values
note: confirmed via Anthropic docs / claude-api skill: Sonnet 5 (and the Claude 5 family) reject temperature/top_p/top_k with a 400

finding: claude-sonnet-5 entry fields/shape byte-identical to the existing claude-opus-4-8 restricted entry
the call-site comment above apply_model_params still read 'defaults preserved when unset', stale after removing the 0.7 default
the thinking-active gate correctly suppresses sampling for thinking-capable-but-allowed models (sonnet-4-6), fixing prior 400s rather than wrongly suppressing valid params
anthropic_http_error/parse_anthropic_error carried a redundant anthropic_ prefix unlike sibling helpers (sampling_restricted, effort_str, ...)
parse_anthropic_error doc said 'Mirrors the SSE-error parse' but SSE uses a typed struct while this helper uses untyped Value access
self-heal wired only into stream_chat; embeddings/upload_file/delete_file still call from_status_code
note: out of scope: those endpoints do not send sampling params, so there is nothing to self-heal

finding: supports_sampling_params field doc enumerated only 'Opus 4.7/4.8', stale after adding sonnet-5
sampling_restricted doc said 'Opus 4.7/4.8 family', stale/disagreeing with the new build_request_body comment listing Sonnet 5
claude-sonnet-5 id omits the minor-version segment used by sibling ids
note: correct: claude-sonnet-5 is Anthropic's actual model id (Sonnet 5 has no minor version)

finding: log messages/comments consistently use 'sampling params (temperature/top_p/top_k)'/'thinking'; no stray ziee-chat/phibya references
loopback self-heal test had no timeout; a connection-reuse regression would hang indefinitely rather than fail cleanly
is_sampling_param_400's raw-body fallback and bare 'sampling' branch were never exercised
note: removed the vague 'sampling' keyword branch; added predicate tests for value-error/unsupported/non-JSON

finding: no loopback test covered the negative retry paths (non-sampling 400 -> exactly one request, clean error; guard against a 2nd retry)
the error body was JSON-parsed twice on the non-repairable path (is_sampling_param_400 + anthropic_http_error)
note: parse once, reuse the parsed pair for both the decision and the clean error

finding: success path cost unchanged: loop breaks on is_success before reading the body; strip mutates in place (no clone)
